skos:Concept.skos:scopeNote: German port on the River Ems.
That portion of the Pacific which stretches from the Arctic Ocean in the north to the equator.

Bordering the five continents of Antarctica, Asia, Australasia, North and South America, the Pacific is the largest of the world's oceans, and covers more area (63,838,000 square miles) than that of the combined seven continents.
River principally lying between the states of Maryland and Virginia, USA, with its outlet to the sea in Chesapeake Bay.
Bordering the continents of Africa, Antarctica, Europe, North and South America the Atlantic is the second largest of the oceans, covering 31,736,000 square miles.
That portion of the Atlantic Ocean which stretches from the Equator in the north to Antarctica.
The land mass comprising, the continents of North and Central America and South America. Use more specific type.
Unable to identify actual place from documentary sources alone.

skos:Concept.skos:prefLabel: Isla Grande De Tierra Del Fuego

skos:Concept.skos:scopeNote: Bordering the continents of Africa, Antarctica, Asia and Australasia, the Indian Ocean covers 28,364,000 square miles.
A sea within a sea, the Andaman Sea consists of that part of the Indian Ocean bordered by Myanmar, Thailand, Malaysia, Sumatra and the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
A sea within a sea, the Arabian Sea consists of the northern part of the Indian Ocean bordered by India, Pakistan, Iran, Oman and Yemen.
An area of the northern Indian Ocean bordered by India, Bangladesh and Myanmar.
Small port on River Trent, North Lincs.
City and port on the Mediterranean coast.
Bordering the continents of Asia, Europe and North America the Arctic Ocean covers 5,426,000 square miles. Situated over the North Pole, much of the ocean remains frozen year round.
That portion of the Atlantic Ocean which stretches from the Arctic Ocean in the north to the Equator.
Unable to identify actual place from documentary sources alone.
A small port town on the coast of northern Lolland.
German city spanning the river Rhine.
Port in Somalia, one of largest in East Africa
Unable to identify actual place from documentary sources alone.
The ocean which surrounds the continent of Antarctica, and includes the most southerly regions of the three largest oceans, the Pacific, Atlantic and Indian.
A sea within a sea, the Aegean consists of that part of the Mediterranean bordered by Greece and Turkey.
Bordered by the continents of Africa, Asia and Europe, the Mediterranean covers some 967,000 sq miles. It is linked to the Atlantic Ocean by the Straits of Gibraltar.
A sea within a sea, the Adriatic consists of that part of the Mediterranean which separates Italy from the Balkans and Greece.
Use only for the historic region of North America. For modern places use NORTH CAROLINA or SOUTH CAROLINA.
Unable to identify actual place from documentary sources alone.
A small port town on the coast of northern Lolland.
Use for the historic region of Flanders only
German port of the western bank of the mouth of the River Weser.
Mascarene Islands, Africa; dependent territory as a departement of France.
That portion of the Pacific which stretches from the equator to Antarctica.
Arlingham civil parish, River Severn, Gloucestershire
Use only for historic state of Czechoslovakia, where new state is known index with CZECH REPUBLIC or SLOVAKIA.
Small port on River Trent, North Lincs.
Arlingham civil parish, River Severn, Gloucestershire
Mascarene Islands, Africa; dependent territory as a departement of France.
Unable to identify actual place from documentary sources alone.
